intent: A full `ausearch -m AVC` event block: the AVC record plus its companion PROCTITLE / PATH / CWD / SYSCALL records, ALL sharing the same audit(TS:SERIAL) = (1780440805.959:40123). Fixtures the parser's companion-record correlation (f4 section 1.3): the bare AVC is self-sufficient for the narrow allow, but the human-meaningful exe=/path=/comm= live in the SYSCALL/PATH records. The parser must (a) group by serial, (b) pull exe="/tmp/avc-test/mycat", path/name="/tmp/avc-test/f1", syscall=257 (openat) for the explanation, and (c) still emit exactly the same narrow allow as the bare AVC. Ships BOTH denials.txt (bare) and denials.ausearch.txt (grouped) - audit2allow yields the identical allow from each.
_kinds_note: TeAllowable; floor none. The companion records carry NO additional decline signal - they enrich the explanation only.

oracle.audit2allow_N: allow logrotate_t shadow_t:file read; (IDENTICAL whether fed the bare denials.txt OR the full denials.ausearch.txt block - audit2allow ignores the companion SYSCALL/PATH/PROCTITLE records for perm generation, confirmed by running both)
oracle.narrower_than_R_by: -R emits the broad auth_read_shadow() macro (attribute + read_file_perms padding + etc_t:dir). Our expected.te emits exactly the single denied read. The companion records do not change the emitted rule; they only enrich the human explanation.

triage_expectation.suspected_trap: Three parser traps. (1) A parser that treats every line as an AVC would choke on the PROCTITLE/PATH/CWD/SYSCALL lines or mis-attribute their k=v fields to the AVC. It must recognize the record TYPE and only the AVC line drives the rule. (2) The PROCTITLE is HEX-ENCODED (proctitle=2f746d70...) - a parser that wants the human cmdline must hex-decode it (NUL-separated argv). (3) Companion correlation must key on the audit(TS:SERIAL) serial (40123) so a multi-event dump groups the right SYSCALL/PATH with the right AVC. The emitted allow must be byte-identical to the bare-AVC case; the exe=/path= are explanation enrichment only, never affecting the rule.
